mutation:
composite: fixed point-clusters -> per-cluster spanning-tree drawing task with a
  global (cross-cluster) non-crossing objective, a vertex angular-resolution objective, and a
  portfolio-level shape-grammar role assignment (PATH/STAR/ZIGZAG) whose fit AND cross-cluster
  diversity are both scored -- an open, no-known-optimum joint layout+assignment problem.
title: Star Atlas Portfolio -- Diverse Constellation Drawing
strategies: trivial: connect each cluster's stars in raw input order as a chain; declare every constellation PATH (baseline, ratio ~0.1)

greedy: per-cluster Euclidean MST (textbook, locally near-optimal, self-crossing-free) with a locally-derived role guess -- fails to diversify the PORTFOLIO because i.i.d.-distributed clusters yield statistically similar MST shapes everywhere

strong: portfolio-level diversity-aware role assignment (balance PATH/STAR/ZIGZAG across clusters using each cluster's PCA elongation as an affinity signal) THEN a role-specific construction recipe per cluster (PCA-axis path / centroid hub-and-spoke star / axis-riffled zigzag) -- exploits the innovation hook directly
open: joint global optimization of angular resolution + cross-cluster non-crossing + role assignment + realized-shape diversity (no known optimal algorithm)
